untranslatable_reason: Source signal references Bulkowski's proprietary 'chart pattern' recognition from Encyclopedia without specifying which pattern type (head-and-shoulders, triangles, rectangles, etc.) or objective entry/exit criteria. The exact pattern formation rules and exit conditions are marked AUTHOR_SILENT. Success rates and specific breakout criteria cannot be operationalized without the pattern definition details. Used pivot crossover as a generic breakout proxy, but this does not capture the actual pattern semantics.
translation_notes: Signal is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT (bullish in bull markets, bearish in bear markets). Breakout direction is BOTH. Data source: 380 formations from 500 stocks (1991-2003). Average rise 29%, average decline 15%. Exit conditions were not specified by author. Pivot points used as a breakout mechanism substitute, but the underlying chart pattern logic (reversal vs continuation identification) cannot be translated to quantitative rules without additional pattern definition.
